title: Blue Man's Group
content: {{Top|ca|leg|italics=1|notoc=1}} {{InUniverseMedia |image= |name=''Blue Man's Group'' |author=[[Tik Tali Talosh]]{{InsiderCite|67|Who's Who in the Max Rebo Band}} |editor= |illustrator= |artist= |performer= |publisher= |release date=During or after [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' establishes the [[Battle of the Great Pit of Carkoon]] occurred in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. Because {{InsiderCite|67|Who's Who in the Max Rebo Band}} reveals the [[Max Rebo Band/Legends|Max Rebo Band]] broke up after that event and [[Tik Tali Talosh]] wrote ''Blue Man's Group'' after the band broke up, the earliest [[Standard year/Legends|year]] for the publication would be 4 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]]. |media type=Biography |pages= |published in= |genre= }} '''''Blue Man's Group''''' was a tell-all biography [[Writing/Legends|written]] by [[Tik Tali Talosh]] about the [[Max Rebo Band/Legends|Max Rebo Band]]. Talosh dreamed about auditioning for his favorite [[Musician/Legends|musician]], [[Max Rebo/Legends|Max Rebo]], as a teenager. A [[harmonica]] player,{{Tales|2|Stop That Jawa!}} Talosh eventually got his opportunity, and the [[Band/Legends|band]] was so impressed by his audition that he was, from [[Time/Legends|time]] to time, invited to perform with them at [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt's]] [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|palace]], on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. The Max Rebo Band broke up after [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY's]] [[Battle of the Great Pit of Carkoon]], where Jabba was [[Death/Legends|killed]]. After the band broke up, Talosh wrote ''Blue Man's Group'' without the Max Rebo Band members' permission. As a result, the band members cut all ties to Talosh, who would forever treasure his brief status as a Max Rebo Band member despite his disappointment. ==Behind the scenes== ''Blue Man's Group'' was mentioned in "[[Who's Who in the Max Rebo Band]]," an article [[Rich Handley]] wrote for the [[Star Wars Insider 67|sixty-seventh issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' magazine, which was published on [[April 15]], [[2003]].{{SWArchive|url=community/news/2003/03/news20030317.html|text=Celebrate Jedi's 20th in ''Insider'' #67}} The name ''Blue Man's Group'' is similar to the real-life [[Wikipedia:Blue Man Group|Blue Man Group]], a performing arts theater group.{{WebCite|url=https://www.savannahnow.com/do/2013-05-08/close-look-inside-secret-life-blue-man|text=A close look inside the secret life of a Blue Man|date=2013-05-08|author=Sickler, Linda|work=[https://www.savannahnow.com savannahnow]|archiveurl=https://archive.ph/FFSEw|archivedate=2022-08-12}} ==Sources== *{{InsiderCite|67|Who's Who in the Max Rebo Band}} {{1stm}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |nl=Blue Man's Group }} [[Category:Journals and memoirs]]
